public_reputation: Kept corpse contagion from becoming civic panic by treating the living and ceremonial dead under one doctrine.
manner: Carries the habits of a place-bound worker whose judgment comes from repetition rather than theory. In Panim, that reads as someone grave, measured, and ritual-aware, with constant awareness of consequence, grief, and divine mediation, and the habits of a healer still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of healer in and around Panim Haven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Panim Haven workable through the obligations of healer while managing the immediate pressure of divine balance obligations in Panim.
hidden_pressure: Must hold local credibility together even when the causes of strain sit elsewhere. Around Panim Haven, that usually means carrying divine balance obligations, sacrificial and mediation decorum, institutional scrutiny without letting the role of healer visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Saint-Physician Orel Vaunt needs help with a problem in Panim Haven that larger systems have pushed down onto ordinary hands.
Getting involved with Saint-Physician Orel Vaunt can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Saint-Physician Orel Vaunt can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

support_scope_notes: Integrated from the prior_release canon-ready named-NPC ledger append. Unsupported fields remain restrained.
public_signal: Saint-Physician Orel Vaunt carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia. In Panim Haven, this lands inside a public field already shaped by House Panim.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Panim Haven: the part of Panim Haven where their work actually matters day to day
who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break
